title: What's new in view transitions (2025 update) | Chrome for Developers
insights: Scoped view transitions: element.startViewTransition() on any HTMLElement. Multiple simultaneous transitions possible. Cross-document transitions in MPA now baseline. Firefox 133+ and Safari 18+ support.

title: View Transition API — MDN Web Docs
insights: document.startViewTransition() captures old/new snapshots, suppresses rendering, powers CSS animations. Cross-document (MPA) and same-document (SPA) modes. Named view-transition-name for shared element transitions. Firefox 144 baseline Nov 2025 — now universally available.

title: prefers-reduced-motion — MDN Web Docs
insights: CSS media query @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce). Covers scale, rotate, wave effects. Fades, dissolves, color-change are safe alternatives. Universal selector * pattern resets transitions site-wide. JS: window.matchMedia() for runtime detection.

title: M3 Expressive: New Motion System
insights: Material 3 Expressive (May 2025) adds spring-like physics motion system tokens. Backed by 46 research studies with 18,000+ participants. Motion is now tokenized system-wide via theme, not hardcoded. Rollout on Android 16/Pixel Q3 2025.

title: Easing and duration — Material Design 3 tokens spec
insights: M3 defines 4 duration groups (short1-4: 50-200ms, medium1-4: 250-400ms, long1-4: 450-600ms, extra-long1-4: 700-1000ms) paired with 5 easing tokens: emphasized (cubic-bezier 0.2,0,0,1.0), emphasizedDecelerate, emphasizedAccelerate, standard, standardDecelerate, standardAccelerate

title: Motion Overview — Material Design 3
insights: M3 motion is physics-informed: uses standard curve for element-to-element, emphasized for expressive moments. Recommends against linear easing. All transitions map to semantic tokens not raw ms values.

title: Motion — JavaScript and React animation library
insights: Formerly Framer Motion, rebranded to Motion in 2025. Hybrid engine: runs via requestAnimationFrame OR Web Animations API (WAAPI). 4KB base, 6KB with React. 120fps capable without React re-renders. motion/react is new import path.

title: AnimatePresence — React exit animations | Motion
insights: AnimatePresence enables exit animations for unmounted components. Requires: (1) wrapper outside conditional, (2) unique key on motion component, (3) motion component as direct child. Without it elements disappear instantly — no exit animation possible in React.

title: Layout Animation — React FLIP and Shared Element | Motion
insights: layout prop auto-animates size changes via FLIP technique. layout='x'/'y' for axis-locked animations. layoutId enables shared element transitions between matching components. layoutScroll required on scrollable ancestors.

title: React Labs: View Transitions, Activity, and more
insights: React Activity component preserves state for hidden elements while allowing enter/exit animation. addTransitionType API for custom animation types based on navigation cause. <ViewTransition name> for shared element transitions.

title: <ViewTransition> — React
insights: React's declarative ViewTransition wraps browser View Transitions API. Animation types: enter (inserted), exit (deleted), update (DOM mutations), share (named matching). Activates only within React transitions (startTransition, useDeferredValue). Experimental as of 2025.

title: Rive — the interactive experience engine
insights: Rive combines design, animation, and logic in one tool. State Machines define interaction-driven transitions. GPU-accelerated vector renderer at 120fps. Data binding for runtime-driven updates. Used by Spotify, Duolingo, Disney, Google. Web/iOS/Android/Flutter/Unity runtimes.

title: Design Tokens Format Module 2025.10 — DTCG
insights: Official DTCG format spec. Token types: color, dimension, fontFamily, fontWeight, duration ($value: number, $type: 'duration'), cubicBezier ($value: [0.25, 0.1, 0.25, 1.0]). Composite tokens for compound motion definitions. Multi-value tokens for transitions.

title: Understanding Success Criterion 2.3.3: Animation from Interactions | WAI/W3C
insights: WCAG 2.3.3 (AAA): Motion animation triggered by interaction must be disableable unless essential. Covers parallax, scroll-triggered motion. Vestibular disorder trigger. Loading indicators and data viz are essential exceptions. CSS prefers-reduced-motion is the standard technique.

title: Design Tokens specification reaches first stable version | W3C DTCG
insights: W3C Design Tokens spec 2025.10 stable release formally introduces duration type (value+unit ms/s) and cubicBezier (array of 4 numbers) as official token types. Motion module in development. First stable version enables motion tokens in Style Dictionary and tooling.

title: FLIP Your Animations — Aerotwist (Paul Lewis)
insights: FLIP: First-Last-Invert-Play. Precalculates animation by reading initial (First) and final (Last) states, inverting transform (Invert), then playing from inverted to identity (Play). Keeps animations on transform/opacity = compositor-only. Foundation for layout animations in every major library.

title: View Transitions API (single-document) | Can I use
insights: Chrome 111+, Edge 111+, Firefox 133+, Safari 18+. Firefox 144 makes it Baseline Newly Available (Oct 2025). Cross-document (MPA) transitions: Chrome 126+, others pending. Same-document universally safe in late 2025.

title: Motion choreography — Carbon Design System
insights: Carbon choreography: focus user during transitional states, provide spatial context, add personality. Timing grid creates consistent rhythm. Animation defined as multiples of base grid unit.

title: Motion — Carbon Design System (IBM)
insights: IBM Carbon defines two motion personalities: productive (faster, professional) and expressive (slower, emotional). Three easing types: standard-easing, entrance-ease (ease-out), exit-easing (ease-in). Six static duration tokens. Duration scales with animation distance/size.

title: spring(duration:bounce:blendDuration:) — Apple Developer Documentation
insights: Swift spring API: duration (perceptual, 0.5s default), bounce (0=critically damped, 1=very bouncy), blendDuration for transition smoothing. Replaces older mass/stiffness/damping API. Matches Figma's design-friendly two-parameter approach.

title: Animate with springs — WWDC 2023 | Apple Developer
insights: SwiftUI spring(duration:bounce:) — perceptual duration stays consistent as bounce changes. Springs are SwiftUI default. Best practice: fix duration for pacing, then adjust bounce for character. @Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion) for accessibility. withAnimation triggers spring automatically.

title: Updates in hardware-accelerated animation capabilities | Chrome
insights: Chrome automatically hardware-accelerates transform and opacity animations. Independent transform properties (translate, scale, rotate) now run on compositor separately. clip-path animations increasingly compositor-eligible. Hardware scroll animations via useScroll/scroll().

title: Create complex animation curves with linear() — Chrome for Developers
insights: linear() CSS easing enables spring, bounce, elastic effects impossible with cubic-bezier(). 88% browser support Oct 2025. Safari does not support. Presets: Spring, Bounce, Material Design emphasized easing. Polyfill available via postcss-spring-easing.

title: Web Animations API — MDN Web Docs
insights: WAAPI: element.animate(keyframes, options). Timing model + Animation model. Keyframes array with offset 0-1. Options: duration, delay, iterations, direction, fill, easing. getAnimations() for introspection. Runs on compositor for transform/opacity. Foundation of Motion library.

title: CSS scroll-driven animations — MDN Web Docs
insights: animation-timeline: scroll() for scroll progress, view() for element viewport visibility. Baseline in Safari 26 (Sept 2025). No JavaScript scroll listeners needed. Hardware accelerated. ScrollTimeline and ViewTimeline WAAPI interfaces for JS control.

title: cubic-bezier() — MDN Web Docs
insights: cubic-bezier(x1,y1,x2,y2) with control points between 0-1. Preset keywords: ease (0.25,0.1,0.25,1), ease-in (0.42,0,1,1), ease-out (0,0,0.58,1), ease-in-out (0.42,0,0.58,1). y values CAN exceed 0-1 for overshoot effects.

title: Motion — Fluent 2 Design System
insights: Fluent 2 motion follows physical laws: inertia, gravity, weight, velocity. Duration + easing defined as global tokens. Linear = constant speed (rotations only). Ease-in for exits, ease-out for entrances, ease-in-out for standard. Micro-animations for WinUI controls. WCAG reduced motion compliant.

title: airbnb/lottie-web — GitHub
insights: Airbnb's open-source Lottie web renderer. Renders After Effects JSON animations. Supports renderer: 'svg', 'canvas', 'html'. 30K+ GitHub stars. Powers web, Android, iOS, React Native animation from single After Effects source.

title: GSAP — GreenSock Animation Platform
insights: GSAP went 100% free April 30 2025 including SplitText, MorphSVG, ScrollTrigger, Flip plugin. Framework-agnostic. Timeline sequences multiple tweens with precise control. Webflow native integration July 2025. Animates CSS, SVG, canvas, React, Vue, WebGL, motion paths.

title: State Machine Overview — Rive
insights: Rive State Machines: visual logic connecting animations. Trigger animations on hover, click, game events. Inputs: Boolean, Number, Trigger. Transitions with conditions and blend modes. Open source runtimes parse .riv files natively.

title: What is a Lottie animation? — LottieFiles
insights: Lottie is JSON-based animation format from After Effects via Bodymovin plugin. Vector-based, scalable, lightweight. dotLottie format (.lottie) = up to 90% smaller than JSON via compression. setQuality() API: high/medium/low performance tradeoff. Files can decrease load times 70%.

title: Changelog — Motion (formerly Framer Motion)
insights: Motion v12.38 (March 2026): oklch/oklab/lab/lch color-mix animation, hardware-accelerated scroll, layoutAnchor prop, axis-locked layout (layout='x'/'y'), axis-locked drag snapping, skipInitialAnimation for useSpring. 290+ examples documented.

title: Animation performance guide | Motion
insights: Only transform and opacity run off main thread. will-change promotes layer prematurely — use sparingly. Motion automatically promotes animated elements. Hardware acceleration via translateZ(0) or will-change: transform.

title: React transitions — Configure Motion animations
insights: Transitions configure HOW animations occur. Spring physics-based: stiffness (default 100), damping (default 10), mass (default 1). Duration-based springs: simpler but no velocity inheritance. Tween type for CSS-style animations with duration + easing.

title: useAnimate — Manual React animation controls | Motion
insights: Imperative animation control scoped to component. Enables sequencing timelines, scrubbing, triggering from outside render cycle. Provides automatic cleanup on unmount. Best for complex choreographed sequences.

title: stagger() — Stagger the delay of multiple animations | Motion
insights: stagger() works via variant delayChildren option. Creates cascading wave effect for lists, grids, nav menus. Can use custom functions based on index for non-linear stagger patterns.

title: The Web Animation Performance Tier List | Motion Magazine
insights: Performance tiers: (S) WAAPI transform/opacity on compositor thread; (A) rAF JS animations; (B) CSS keyframes; (C) layout-affecting props. Motion's hybrid engine auto-selects WAAPI when possible, falls back to rAF for complex values.

title: Variants — Motion Tutorial
insights: Variants are reusable animation state definitions. Parent propagates animation state to children automatically. Enables staggerChildren, delayChildren orchestration without prop drilling. Custom prop passes dynamic variant values.

title: View transitions guide — Next.js
insights: Next.js App Router: <ViewTransition> integrates with startTransition for route animations. template.tsx resets animation state on route change (unlike layout.tsx). Exit animations work in template.tsx. useNavigate with viewTransition:true for programmatic navigation.

title: Using motion — Shopify Polaris React
insights: Motion use cases: loading indicators (system processing feedback), action feedback (confirm merchant actions), navigation guidance (context continuity). Consistent with Admin experience standards.

title: Motion tokens — Shopify Polaris
insights: Polaris mandates use of motion tokens over custom styles for Admin consistency. Stylelint rules disallow raw ms values in transition/animation — must use tokens. Disallows CSS @keyframes directly. Focus: loading states, feedback, navigation context.

title: useTransition — React Spring
insights: Physics-based React animation. useSpring for single values, useTrail for cascading, useTransition for mount/unmount lifecycle, useChain for sequences, useSprings for multiple. No fixed duration — springs naturally settle. Velocity inheritance enables natural interruption.

title: View Transitions — React Router
insights: React Router: useNavigate with { viewTransition: true } enables View Transitions API on navigation. Works with named view-transition-name for shared elements. <Link viewTransition> for declarative navigation with transitions.

title: Motion — Adobe Spectrum Design System
insights: Spectrum defines 3 custom easing curves with CSS, JS, iOS and Android code plus After Effects keyframe velocities. Motion should be purposeful, intuitive, seamless. Tokens provided for each curve. Supports platform-specific implementations.

title: Measure performance with the RAIL model — web.dev
insights: RAIL: Response (<100ms), Animation (<10ms per frame, ~16.7ms budget at 60fps), Idle (50ms chunks), Load (<1s). Animation budget is the strictest. Browser needs ~6ms to render — leaves ~10ms for JS work per frame. Jank = dropped frames.

title: Animation and motion | web.dev
insights: Avoid animations >5s without pause control. No content flashing >3 times/second. Use fade/dissolve instead of scale/rotate for motion-sensitive users. Test with OS accessibility settings: Win: Visual effects off; Mac: Reduce motion on.

title: React Animation — Keyframes, Transitions and Gestures | Framer Motion
insights: Motion (formerly Framer Motion) v12.38. animate prop for declarative animations, initial/exit for AnimatePresence. Keyframes as arrays. Gestures: whileHover, whileTap, whileDrag, whileFocus. Hardware-accelerated up to 120fps. No React re-renders on animation.

title: An Interactive Guide to CSS Transitions — Josh W. Comeau
insights: Comprehensive interactive guide to CSS transitions. Timing functions: nothing in nature moves linearly. Property performance: height expensive (layout), transform/opacity cheap (compositor). hardware acceleration via will-change: transform. Covers transition-property, timing, delay.

title: An Interactive Guide to CSS Keyframe Animations — Josh W. Comeau
insights: @keyframes animation vs transitions. fill-mode: forwards for end-state persistence. animation-iteration-count: infinite for continuous. Stagger via animation-delay. Performance considerations: only transform+opacity for compositor path.

title: Springs and Bounces in Native CSS — Josh W. Comeau
insights: Using CSS linear() to approximate spring physics without JavaScript. Records Svelte spring steps, converts to linear() curve points. Practical implementation with fallback for Safari. Tool: https://www.kvin.me/css-springs for generation.

title: Including Animation In Your Design System — Smashing Magazine
insights: 5-step process: (1) define motion principles, (2) establish duration/easing vocabulary, (3) define building blocks (fade, slide, scale), (4) document choreography patterns, (5) prepare dev handoff specs. Pencil Design System (Brainly) cited as best example: duration scale, choreography guidelines, accessibility, duration calculator.

title: Motion — Ant Design
insights: Ant Motion: animations backed by laws of nature for smooth feel. Enterprise-level UX requires minimal transition time. Uses entrance easing (ease-out), exit easing (ease-in), standard easing (ease-in-out), linear. Tokens: name given to durations like 200ms = 'fast'.

title: Motion — Atlassian Design System
insights: Atlassian provides motion utilities for entering/exiting animations. Choreography: multiple objects in motion simultaneously. Focuses user during transitions, provides spatial context, adds personality. FadeIn, SlideIn components with configurable duration and easing tokens.

title: Motion — Entering motions — Atlassian Design System
insights: Atlaskit motion package provides EnteringMotion, ExitingPersistence components. Configures stagger for lists. Duration and easing tokens available via motion/variables. React hooks for animation state management.

title: AutoAnimate — Add motion with a single line of code
insights: Zero-config 1.9KB library. Observes DOM mutations, auto-applies FLIP technique. Works with React, Vue, Svelte, vanilla JS. useAutoAnimate hook. Retroactively applicable to any markup including third-party. Disables on prefers-reduced-motion automatically.

title: Shared Element Transitions — React Native Reanimated
insights: sharedTransitionTag prop links components across screens. Animates matching elements during navigation. Works with React Navigation. Interpolates position, size, opacity between source and destination.

title: Easing Functions Cheat Sheet
insights: Visual catalog of 30+ easing functions with cubic-bezier values and interactive previews. Categories: easeIn, easeOut, easeInOut variants (Sine, Quad, Cubic, Quart, Quint, Expo, Circ, Back, Elastic, Bounce). Essential reference for motion token definition.

title: ant-design/ant-motion — Animation specification and components
insights: Ant Design's animation package with React components and specification. Open source motion design guidelines as code. Enterprise animation vocabulary.

title: formkit/auto-animate — GitHub
insights: 3.6K+ GitHub stars. Drop-in animation for any JS framework. Applies add/remove/reorder animations automatically via MutationObserver + FLIP. No configuration required for basic use. Custom animation override available.

title: Flip Plugin — GSAP
insights: GSAP Flip plugin implements FLIP technique declaratively. Captures state, changes layout, animates from captured to new. Works with CSS Grid, Flexbox rearrangements. More robust than manual FLIP for complex reorders.

title: Should I use Framer Motion or Motion One? | Motion Blog
insights: Motion One (motion package) = 3.8KB animate() vs Framer Motion 17KB. WAAPI-first = compositor thread by default. Better for mobile-heavy projects. Framer Motion has richer gesture support (drag, hover, tap). Choose by bundle budget vs interaction richness.

title: CSS animation generation — CSS springs | Motion
insights: Motion's CSS animation generator produces optimized keyframes for use without the library. Converts spring physics to @keyframes + linear() CSS. Enables Motion-designed animations in CSS-only contexts or as static export.

title: AnimateActivity — Create React exit animations | Motion
insights: AnimateActivity preserves component state during exit animations. Useful for tabs, routes that should maintain scroll position while animating out. Works with AnimatePresence for complete lifecycle management.

title: Improvements to Web Animations API | Motion
insights: Motion adds: independent transform animations (translateX separate from rotate), CSS variable animation, color animation (oklch/oklab/lab/lch/color-mix). WAAPI gaps filled by Motion's hybrid engine. Hardware scroll animations via useScroll.

title: Should I use Framer Motion or Motion One? | Motion Magazine
insights: Decision guide: Motion One (motion) for performance-critical, content sites, PWAs, bundle-sensitive. Framer Motion (motion/react) for gesture-heavy, complex layout, design tool integration. Both from same team, same package now.

title: Spring — Popmotion
insights: Popmotion is the physics engine underlying Framer Motion. Spring accepts mass, velocity, stiffness, damping. Physics module adds velocity, acceleration, friction. Decay for momentum-scroll feel. Higher stiffness = snappier, higher mass = more lethargic.

title: Motion — Seeds Design System (Sprout Social)
insights: Seeds uses distance-based duration selection: ≤25% view width = fast token, 26-50% = medium, 51-100% = slow. Easing: ease-in for exits (dropped penny), ease-out for entrances (hit baseball), ease-in-out for standard. Fade uses fast token for buttons/list items.

title: animation — Tailwind CSS
insights: Built-in: animate-spin, animate-ping, animate-pulse, animate-bounce. Tailwind v4: custom keyframes in @theme block in CSS (not JS config). Skeleton loaders via animate-pulse. Ping for notification badges. All respect motion-reduce: modifier.

title: transition-duration — Tailwind CSS
insights: Duration utilities: 75ms, 100ms, 150ms, 200ms, 300ms, 500ms, 700ms, 1000ms. Arbitrary values: duration-[400ms]. Default transition-duration: 150ms. Stagger achievable via custom delay-* utilities.

title: transition-property — Tailwind CSS
insights: Tailwind transition utilities: transition (all), transition-colors, transition-transform, transition-opacity, transition-shadow, transition-none. Duration utilities: duration-75 to duration-1000. Timing: ease-in, ease-out, ease-in-out, linear. motion-safe: and motion-reduce: modifier variants built-in.

title: Motion — Norton Design System
insights: Norton implements Material Design motion system. duration-scalar CSS custom property multiplies all durations (set to 0 when prefers-reduced-motion). Automatically respects accessibility with zero configuration. Uses @wwnds/core library. Semantic token names.

title: How Figma put the bounce in spring animations | Figma Blog
insights: Figma's spring animation UX: two-handle approach (bounce + duration) instead of stiffness/damping/mass. More intuitive for designers. Duration = perceptual duration that stays stable as bounce changes. Translates to physics parameters internally.

title: Skeleton Screens 101 — Nielsen Norman Group
insights: NNGroup research: skeleton screens feel faster than spinner for long loads (>1s). Not always better for quick loads. Should reflect actual content layout. Progressive reveal (skeleton→content) more natural than instant swap. Use with placeholders that match content dimensions.

title: Skeleton loading screen design — How to improve perceived performance | LogRocket
insights: Use skeleton when loading >3s. Two animations: pulse (attention-grabbing) and shimmer/wave (best for perceived duration — slow left-to-right wave). Keep same layout structure as real content. Don't extend load time for animation. Matches content shape for seamless skeleton→content transition.

title: Advanced animation patterns with Framer Motion — Maxime Heckel
insights: Advanced patterns: shared layout animations, layout groups, AnimatePresence with mode='wait'/'popLayout'. Custom transition per variant state. propagate prop for parent→child orchestration. Particle effects and morphing with layoutId.

title: The physics behind spring animations — Maxime Heckel
insights: Derives spring differential equation: F = -kx - bv. Mass-spring-damper system. Under/over/critically damped. Stiffness k controls oscillation frequency, damping b controls decay rate. Framer Motion defaults: k=100, b=10, m=1. Explains why velocity inheritance feels natural.

title: Design accessible animation and movement — Pope Tech
insights: Dec 2025: Replace scale/rotate/wave with fade/dissolve/color-change for reduced-motion. Both CSS @media and JS matchMedia needed for CSS and JS animations. Animations >5s need pause mechanism. No flashing >3x/second. Test on Windows Accessibility settings.

title: Investigate Animation Performance with Chrome DevTools | Calibre
insights: DevTools: Performance tab → frame rate chart → green = composite (fast), yellow = JS, purple = layout. Layers panel shows promoted GPU layers. will-change visible in Rendering panel. Identifies jank: layout thrashing, forced reflows, paint storms.

title: prefers-reduced-motion — CSS-Tricks
insights: CSS-Tricks almanac entry. Universal selector pattern: * { animation-duration: 0.01ms; transition-duration: 0.01ms } for blanket reduction. No: { animation: none } as it removes state. Discussion of reduce vs no-preference values and designing for both.

title: Ten tips for better CSS transitions and animations — Josh Collinsworth
insights: 10 tips: (1) don't animate layout properties, (2) use animation-fill-mode, (3) stagger with delay, (4) use custom properties for dynamic transitions, (5) respect prefers-reduced-motion, (6) don't transition everything on hover, (7) use will-change sparingly, (8) watch for transition conflicts.

title: Animation/Motion Design Tokens for complex Design Systems | Medium
insights: 3-tier motion token structure: primitives (raw values), semantic (intent-based), component (specific usage). Encapsulate easing+duration into single intent token (e.g., 'motion-enter-standard'). Enables choreography without worrying about raw values. Style Dictionary transformation patterns.

title: Improve the payment experience with animations — Stripe/Medium
insights: Stripe's animation philosophy: animations help users understand flow between states. Animate individual UI elements to prevent context loss. Prioritize compositor-only properties. CSS transitions fastest for hover/state. Brand-aligned subtle motion for emotional reinforcement. Off main thread work = 60/120fps.

title: Working Together: UX Motion Specs for Design and Development — IBM Design
insights: IBM motion spec process: designers create annotated motion specs with easing curves, duration values, AfterEffects previews. Developers implement from spec using carbon-motion package. Shared vocabulary: productive vs expressive, entrance vs standard vs exit easing.

title: Disney's 12 principles of animation in UX design — UX Collective
insights: Disney's 12 principles applied to UI: squash+stretch for tactile button feedback, staging for directing focus during transitions, follow-through for natural element sequences, anticipation before major actions (subtle pre-animation), timing as core usability factor. Foundation for purposeful motion vocabulary.

title: 5 steps for including motion design in your design system — designsystems.com
insights: Step 1: decide scope (micro vs macro). Step 2: establish motion principles aligned to brand. Step 3: define primitive building blocks (fade-in, slide-right, scale-up). Step 4: choreography patterns from primitives. Step 5: dev handoff with annotation tools, motion specs, duration calculators.

title: Animating View Transitions — patterns.dev
insights: Patterns.dev guide on View Transitions. Custom CSS animations via ::view-transition-old and ::view-transition-new pseudo-elements. view-transition-name for named transitions. Cross-document with @view-transition rule. Practical SPA patterns with React Router and Next.js.

title: The Path To Awesome CSS Easing With linear() — Smashing Magazine
insights: Deep dive into linear() function syntax, how to define curve points, use SVG paths as source. Tool ecosystem around linear() generation. Browser support timeline. The easing function that unlocks everything cubic-bezier() cannot do.

title: Ambient Animations In Web Design: Principles and Implementation (Part 1) — Smashing Magazine
insights: Ambient animations: passive, slow, non-interruptive background motion. 5 layers each at different speeds creates perceived depth. Natural motion cues: does it have weight? Is it flexible? CSS transforms + opacity preferred for performance. prefers-reduced-motion mandatory check.

title: Ambient Animations In Web Design: Practical Applications (Part 2) — Smashing Magazine
insights: Part 2: practical implementation of ambient animations. Techniques: CSS gradients in motion, particle systems, SVG path animations, WebGL backgrounds. Performance budgets for ambient vs interactive motion. Seamless loop design patterns.

title: Animating with Physics — ishandeveloper
insights: Physics animation vs duration-based: spring animations never 'complete' until at rest (velocity < threshold). Enables natural interruption — current velocity preserved on new animation. Three systems: spring (oscillate), decay (momentum), tween (duration-based).

title: 12 Micro Animation Examples Bringing Apps to Life in 2025
insights: 2025 micro-interaction catalog: button state confirmations (200ms), form validation feedback, notification badge ping, loading skeleton shimmer, toggle switch spring, pull-to-refresh physics, like/heart particle burst, typing indicator bounce. 120-220ms optimal for micro-animations.

title: Motion — hover.to Design System
insights: Small design system with documented motion token structure: instant (100ms), fast (160ms), base (240ms), slow (360ms). Spring tokens with stiffness/damping variants. Example of real-world motion token tier implementation.

title: CSS Scroll-Driven Animations Guide — design.dev
insights: Complete guide: scroll-timeline CSS property, animation-timeline: scroll(root) / scroll(nearest). @scroll-timeline vs CSS function syntax. view() animation for element entrance on scroll. Named timelines for multiple animations sharing one scroll container.

title: Motion Tokens: naming your movement — Francesco Improta
insights: Motion token naming conventions: semantic names convey intent (motion-enter-smooth, motion-exit-quick) rather than raw values. Distinguishes between primitive (100ms, cubic-bezier), alias (motion-speed-fast), and semantic (motion-dialog-enter) layers.

title: React Native Reanimated 3: High-Performance Animations in 2025
insights: Reanimated 4 (mid-2025): all animations run on UI thread — blocked JS thread doesn't affect animation smoothness. Worklets execute JS on native thread. Shared Values drive reactive UI updates. CSS animations + worklet-based unified in v4.

title: Rive vs Lottie: Which Animation Tool to Use in 2025? — DEV Community
insights: Decision matrix: Lottie = AE designers, existing workflows, simpler playback, JSON portability. Rive = interactive/stateful, real-time logic, GPU renderer, smaller runtime. Lottie 60% smaller than GIF; Rive 2-3x faster rendering via GPU vector. Rive state machines vs Lottie segments.

title: okikio/spring-easing — GitHub
insights: Generates spring animation frame arrays for use with GSAP, AnimeJS, Framer Motion, Motion One, WAAPI. Converts spring physics to linear() CSS easing. PostCSS plugin available. Works as polyfill for Safari's lack of linear() support.

title: Animating React UIs in 2025: Framer Motion 12 vs React Spring 10
insights: Comparison: Motion (FM12) — more intuitive API, better TypeScript, layout animations, WAAPI hybrid, richer gesture support. React Spring 10 — truer physics, no fixed duration, better for continuous gesture-driven animations. Both production-stable in 2025.

title: A complete guide to CSS Scroll-driven Animations | Mariana Beldi
insights: Comprehensive CSS scroll-driven guide. animation-range: entry/exit/contain/cover. view() with inset for viewport margins. Multiple simultaneous scroll-driven animations. Interaction with reduced-motion via @media prefers-reduced-motion.

title: Rive: Animate Web UIs with State Machines — Marmelab
insights: Practical Rive state machine integration in React. @rive-app/react-canvas package. useRive hook for state machine inputs. Boolean inputs toggle states, number inputs for progress, triggers for one-shot events. Practical loading indicator and interactive button examples.

title: Disney's Motion Principles in Designing Interface Animations | Marvel
insights: Anticipation: pre-animation cue before major action (button slight press before full action). Exaggeration: intentional overstatement for clarity (error shake). Staging: use motion to direct attention hierarchy during complex transitions. Appeal: personality through natural timing.

title: Motion Design System — A Practical Guide | Medium
insights: Atomic approach: atoms (fade, slide, scale), molecules (modal enter = fade+scale), organisms (page transition = slide+stagger). Document animation vocabulary before implementation. Handoff: motion specs with easing graphs, duration values, timing diagrams.

title: Disney's motion principles in interface animations — Medium
insights: Practical application: follow-through = staggered child animations after parent stops. Overlapping action = offset timing between related elements. Secondary animation = supplementary motion reinforcing primary action. Squash+stretch = scale feedback on interactive elements.

title: Micro Interactions That Feel Magical — netcodesign
insights: Pattern library: hover lift (translateY -2px, box-shadow), click ripple (scale 0→1 from click point), success checkmark draw (SVG stroke-dashoffset), loading dots (bounce stagger 150ms offset), input focus glow (box-shadow spring). All under 300ms.

title: Intro to the Web Animations API — Pawel Grzybek
insights: Practical WAAPI introduction. element.animate() syntax. KeyframeEffect constructor. Animation.play()/pause()/reverse(). getAnimations() for inspection. Performance: WAAPI runs on compositor for transform/opacity. Comparison with CSS transitions and requestAnimationFrame approach.

title: Framer Motion React Animations: Complete Guide | Refine
insights: Comprehensive guide covering motion components, variants, AnimatePresence, layout animations, spring physics, gestures. Good code examples for stagger, page transitions, drag interactions. Practical reference for React developers.

title: List Animation using Motion for React — theodorusclarence
insights: 4 rules for list enter/exit: need 2 wrapper divs, margins/gaps not auto-animated with height, components must forward ref, items inside AnimatePresence need keys. Height animation: parent gets layout prop, children get individual animations.

title: Integrating Rive into a React Project — Codrops 2025
insights: Production Rive integration case study. Artboard management for multiple animation scenes. State machine inputs via React state. Performance: lazy load runtimes, use canvas renderer. Data binding in v2 for reactive values. GPU renderer vs SVG for complex scenes.

title: High-Performing Lottie Animations on the Web — 8awake
insights: File size is primary Lottie performance factor. Use dotLottie converter (90% size reduction). Avoid excessive DOM nodes from complex JSON. Use renderer: svg for quality, canvas for performance. Lazy load off-screen Lottie instances. Use autoplay: false until intersection.

title: 60 FPS Performant Web Animations — Algolia Engineering
insights: 16.7ms frame budget. Composite-only properties (transform, opacity) = paint-free. Layout thrashing avoidance: batch DOM reads before writes. requestAnimationFrame synchronization. Layer promotion strategy vs memory cost. Practical optimization checklist.

title: Effortless UI Spring Animations: A Two-Parameter Approach
insights: Duration + bounce as designer-friendly spring parameters. CSS Spring Generator at kvin.me/css-springs. Maps to linear() output for native CSS. Aligns with Figma's and Apple's spring UI (duration+bounce vs mass+stiffness+damping).

title: Creating CSS Spring Animations With Linear Easing Function — PQINA
insights: Step-by-step: use Svelte spring → capture steps → convert to linear() → apply as CSS transition. Generates easing like linear(0, 0.0049, ..., 0.9957, 1). Works today in Chrome/Firefox; Safari fallback graceful.

title: Framer Motion vs Motion One: Mobile Animation Performance in 2025
insights: Mobile test results: Motion One WAAPI-first = consistent 60fps on mid-range devices. Framer Motion JS-driven = occasional jank on low-end. For multiple simultaneous animations, Motion One 85% smaller bundle wins on mobile. Complex gestures still favor Framer Motion.

title: Design Tokens for Motion: How to Standardize Animation | Ruixen
insights: Centralizing durations, delays, easings as CSS custom properties: --dur-quick, --eas-out-strong. Using @property for type-safe interpolation. Three levels: primitives, semantic, component-specific. Enables global motion speed adjustment via single token.

title: Physics-based animations spring to life — Nordcraft Blog
insights: Visual builder approach to spring physics. Real-time spring parameter adjustment. Explains how non-physicists can tune animations: start with duration, add bounce, observe natural feel. Aligns with Apple/Figma's designer-friendly parameter approach.

title: How to Create Physics-Based Spring Animations with Custom Damping — DEV Community
insights: Custom spring implementation in JavaScript. Hooke's Law F=-kx. Euler integration. Damping coefficient prevents infinite oscillation. Code example for requestAnimationFrame-based spring loop.

title: Creating Skeleton UI Loading Animation — ProtoPie
insights: Skeleton animation techniques in ProtoPie: gradient mask moving left-to-right (shimmer). Key: 200-500ms timing, match content layout exactly, transition skeleton→content with fade (no flicker). Shows how to prototype skeleton→content transition for handoff.
